title: Iran census history and demographic statistics, 1868-1998, extracted from Encyclopaedia Iranica's 'CENSUS i. In Iran' and 'DEMOGRAPHY' narrative articles
source_org: Encyclopaedia Iranica Foundation (articles), citing Bharier (1968, 1972), Amani (1988), Amirahmadi, Aghajanian (1992), Bozorgmehr and Sabbagh, Chasteland et al., Jalali et al., Moore et al., and primary Markaz-e Amar-e Iran (Statistical Centre of Iran) census bulletins as original sources
extraction_method: Both live iranicaonline.org URLs return HTTP 403 (Cloudflare bot-block), consistent with every other agent's experience with this site in this project. Retrieved via Wayback Machine archived snapshots instead (CENSUS i. In Iran: http://web.archive.org/web/20120107163945id_/http://www.iranicaonline.org/articles/census-i ; DEMOGRAPHY: http://web.archive.org/web/20120106112202id_/http://www.iranicaonline.org/articles/demography), stripped HTML tags, and manually transcribed every year-tagged statistic explicitly stated in the article's prose, following the project's established iranica-fiscal-system-narrative-series precedent (data.csv: year_or_period, metric, value, unit, cited_source, with the article's own citation to its underlying academic/primary source preserved). The DEMOGRAPHY article is a compound Encyclopaedia Iranica entry covering the whole Iranian-cultural-sphere region (it continues into separate Afghanistan and Tajikistan subsections after the Iran section ends) -- ONLY the Iran-specific subsection (which discusses Markaz-e Amar-e Iran, Persia, Tehran, Shiraz, Isfahan, etc.) was extracted; the Afghanistan and Tajikistan subsections were explicitly excluded to avoid misattributing their statistics to Iran. The articles reference numbered tables (Table 10 through Table 23) and figures that illustrate the same data graphically/tabularly, but these render as embedded images in the original site and were NOT captured by the archived HTML snapshot (no <table> tags present in the archived source) -- only the prose-stated figures were recoverable, which is what this dataset contains. No number was calculated, interpolated, or estimated beyond what the source text states.
retrieved_by: claude (manual transcription from Wayback-Machine-archived Encyclopaedia Iranica article text)
time_coverage: 1868-1998 (irregular: pre-modern local head-counts, all 4 Pahlavi/early-Islamic-Republic national censuses 1956/1966/1976/1986, and demographic-survey/estimate years through the mid-1990s)
frequency: irregular -- whatever periodization the original articles used
license_terms: Encyclopaedia Iranica -- academic reference, cite the article
notes: MISSION CONTEXT: this is the highest-value secondary-citation find of this pass. The 'CENSUS i. In Iran' article is a detailed institutional/methodological history of Iran's 1956, 1966, 1976, and 1986 censuses (who ran them, how many enumerators, exact date ranges, publication format down to the number of fascicles) written by demographic historians with full citations to the original Markaz-e Amar-e Iran bulletin numbers -- e.g. the 1966 census results were published as Markaz-e Amar-e Iran, Saršomari-e omumi-e nofus wa maskan-e Aban-mah-e 1345, no. 168 (Esfand 1346/Feb-Mar 1967). These exact bulletin citations (full list in the article's bibliography, not transcribed into data.csv but visible in the archived HTML at data/raw/.../ if ever needed) are a ready-made lead for a future human-outreach step to a research library that might hold physical copies. KEY CROSS-VALIDATIONS: the 1956 definitive total (18,954,704), 1976 total (33,708,744), and 1986 total (49,445,000/49,445,010) all match the UN Demographic Yearbook Historical Supplement Table 2 figures obtained independently in this same pass (see ../../un-demographic-yearbook-historical/) to the exact digit or within rounding -- strong three-way confirmation (Iranica narrative / WB archival PDF / UN DYB) that these are the correct, authoritative definitive census counts. KNOWN DISCREPANCY flagged, not resolved: Bharier's (1968) independently-estimated census under-enumeration rates (7.5% for 1956, 5.0% for 1966, 2.5% for 1976) are notably higher than the Statistical Centre of Iran's own official adjustment factors (~2% for 1956, ~1% for 1966) cited in the World Bank's 1974 statistical appendix (see ../../world-bank-archives-iran/census-demographic-citations-1956-1982/) -- both recorded per this project's bias-policy on contested estimates rather than picking one.

failures.note: Consistent with every other agent's prior experience fetching iranicaonline.org directly in this project; worked around via Wayback Machine archived snapshot instead
Same as above; worked around via Wayback Machine
